---
title = "Navigation"
---

# Prev / Next

*Calepin* websites automatically display "Previous" and "Next" links at the bottom of each page. The order of these links is governed by the `[[contents]]` array in `_calepin/config.toml`.

Pages appear in the prev/next sequence in the same order they are listed in `[[contents]]`. For example, given this configuration:

```toml
[[contents]]
include = ["pages/installation.qmd", "pages/getting_started.qmd"]

[[contents]]
text = "Guide"
include = [
  "guide/intro.qmd",
  "guide/advanced.qmd",
]
```

the navigation order is:

1. `pages/installation.qmd`
2. `pages/getting_started.qmd`
3. `guide/intro.qmd`
4. `guide/advanced.qmd`

The "Previous" link on `guide/intro.qmd` points to `pages/getting_started.qmd`, and its "Next" link points to `guide/advanced.qmd`. Section boundaries are crossed seamlessly.

Pages marked `standalone = true` are excluded from the prev/next chain:

```toml
[[contents]]
standalone = true
include = ["index.qmd", "404.qmd"]
```

## Template variables

In collection templates, the prev/next links are available as:

- {% raw %}`{{ document.prev.text }}`{% endraw %} and {% raw %}`{{ document.prev.href }}`{% endraw %}
- {% raw %}`{{ document.next.text }}`{% endraw %} and {% raw %}`{{ document.next.href }}`{% endraw %}

Both are empty when there is no previous or next page.
